title: Young Gun: Riley Drage is researching the 'hidden damage' in our soils
description_clean: "You've got to get back to your roots and see what people think."
summary: Young researcher Riley Drage is studying hidden damage in soils, emphasizing the importance of returning to fundamentals and understanding community perspectives.

title: Rare event as SAM shifts NZ’s weather pattern
description_clean: Southern Annular Mode reminds us that our weather is influenced by factors beyond El Niño, says Phil Duncan. The post Rare event as SAM shifts NZ’s weather pattern appeared first on Farmers Weekly .
summary: According to Phil Duncan, the Southern Annular Mode (SAM) is shifting New Zealand's weather patterns in a rare event, highlighting that weather is influenced by factors beyond El Niño.

title: ‘It’s straight back to the farm’ after national title win

summary: Tom Slee won the FMG Young Farmer of the Year title, continuing a family tradition and returning the national farming title to the Otago Southland region.

title: 'Big-f' vs 'little-f' farmer: why more women on farm are forging a new path

title: 'I'm here to make money': why this dairy veteran is now banking on Angus beef

title: Gene tech is not devastating the organics industry

title: Waikato Bay of Plenty sweeps youth farming titles

summary: Waikato Bay of Plenty dominated youth farming titles, with a Year 10 duo winning the FMG Junior Young Farmer of the Year and Otewa School claiming the AgriKidsNZ championship at the Grand Final in New Plymouth.
